editorial.title: The great attention deficit: from council flip-flops to colonel sanders' identity crisis
editorial.teaser: While nsw rushes to diagnose everyone with adhd, professor remy suggests the northern beaches council—and perhaps kfc’s product development team—should be first in line for treatment.
editorial.body:
If you’ve glanced at the nsw headlines this week, you’d be forgiven for thinking the entire state is collectively searching for its car keys. The news cycle is dominated by a push for a national approach to adhd, with gps now empowered to diagnose adults to cut wait times.

experts are pointing out symptoms everywhere. But i submit to you that if we are looking for textbook examples of executive dysfunction, impulsivity, and an inability to stick to a plan, we need look no further than the northern beaches council chambers.

the local government is currently exhibiting behavior that would worry even the most lenient psychiatrist. First, they backflipped on housing rules to entice developers to a "failed" suburb, leaving everyone in limbo.

then, the lec had to reverse the decision regarding seniors. The council is performing more rapid directional changes than a frightened toddler in the local kids' triathlon.

they can't seem to sit still, focus on a policy, or finish a task without suddenly reversing course. Perhaps the gps should head to the council meetings first?

but the inability to focus isn't just local; it’s a national malaise. Take kfc, for instance.

they have released a "bánh mì." this is a culinary identity crisis of the highest order. It has the name, but not the nature—much like barnaby joyce, who retains the title of a statesman while his scandals suggest he should perhaps seek a "change of scenery."

we are living in an era of pure distraction.

anger is erupting at volunteer lifesavers (impulse control issues, anyone?), and jetstar has cancelled 90 flights because the planes, much like our politicians, have been recalled for being faulty. We are stuck on the tarmac, eating fake vietnamese sandwiches, watching our council spin in circles.

my diagnosis? we all need a ritalin and a nap.
editorial.author: Professor Remy
